# read_vec { #neuroim.read_vec }

```python
read_vec(filename, indices=None, mask=None)
```

Read a 4D neuroimaging vector from file.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type                                                   | Description                                                     | Default    |
|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| filename | [str](`str`) or [Path](`pathlib.Path`)                 | Path to the 4D neuroimaging file                                | _required_ |
| indices  | [array](`array`) - [like](`like`)                      | Indices of volumes to load (0-based) If None, loads all volumes | `None`     |
| mask     | [LogicalNeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.LogicalNeuroVol`) | Mask for sparse representation                                  | `None`     |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                                       | Description          |
|--------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
|        | [DenseNeuroVec](`neuroim.neuro_vec.DenseNeuroVec`) or [SparseNeuroVec](`neuroim.neuro_vec.SparseNeuroVec`) | The loaded 4D vector |

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

Unlike R which uses 1-based indexing, Python uses 0-based indexing
for the indices parameter.

## R Equivalent {.doc-section .doc-section-r-equivalent}

neuroim2::read_vec
